info.title: Harmony API
info.description:
REST API for Harmony.

**Rate Limiting:** All endpoints are subject to rate limiting. When the limit is exceeded, the API responds with `429 Too Many Requests` and a `Retry-After` header indicating when the client may retry.

get.summary: Deep health check endpoint.
Liveness probe — confirms the process is running and can serve HTTP.
get.description: No dependency checks (DB, cache, etc.). Use `/health` for deep readiness.

get.summary: Check whether a username is available for registration.
get.description:
Public endpoint (no auth required) — used during signup to give instant feedback.
Validates format, checks reserved list, content filter, and queries the database.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on database failure.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The username to check for availability.

post.summary: Create a one-time desktop auth code (browser side of the PKCE exchange).
post.description:
The authenticated user's access token is extracted from the Authorization header,
and the refresh token + PKCE `code_challenge` are provided in the request body.
Returns a short-lived auth code that the desktop app can redeem.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on missing Authorization header, validation failure, or DB error.

post.summary: Redeem a one-time desktop auth code for session tokens.
post.description:
Verifies the PKCE `code_verifier` against the stored `code_challenge` (S256),
then returns the access and refresh tokens. The auth code is consumed
(deleted) in a single atomic query to guarantee single-use.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure, expired/invalid code, or PKCE mismatch.

post.summary: Sync (get or create) the authenticated user's profile.
post.description:
Called after Supabase login. Creates a profile row if this is the first login,
or returns the existing one.

Username resolution order:
1. `user_metadata.username` from the JWT (set during signup)
2. Fallback: derived from the email prefix

All username policy (reserved names, content filter, safe fallback for
system-derived names) is handled by `ProfileService::upsert_from_auth`.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if the JWT lacks an email claim, the username is reserved
or offensive (user-chosen only), or the upsert fails.

delete.summary: Soft-delete a message. Only the author can delete (ADR-038).
delete.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on authorization failure or repository error.

patch.summary: Edit a message's content. Only the author can edit.
patch.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure, authorization failure, or repository error.

post.summary: Add a reaction to a message.
post.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure, authorization failure, or repository error.

delete.summary: Remove a reaction from a message.
delete.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on authorization failure or repository error.

post.summary: Register a Megolm session for an encrypted channel. Requires channel membership.
post.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if the channel is not encrypted, or on repository error.

get.summary: List messages in a channel with cursor-based pagination.
get.description:
Use `before` (ISO 8601) to paginate backward. Default limit is 50, max is 100.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if the cursor is invalid or a repository error occurs.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp cursor — fetch messages created before this time.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of messages to return (1-100, default 50).

post.summary: Send a message to a channel.
post.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

get.summary: Get notification settings for a channel.
get.description:
Returns the default level (`all`) when no explicit setting exists.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on repository error.

patch.summary: Update notification settings for a channel.
patch.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

patch.summary: Mark a channel as read up to a specific message.
patch.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

post.summary: Signal that the authenticated user is typing in a channel.
post.description:
Validates channel existence and server membership, then emits a
`TypingStarted` event via the event bus. No database write — purely
ephemeral relay.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if the channel is not found, the user is not a
member, or the profile lookup fails.

post.summary: Join a voice channel. Returns a `LiveKit` token for the client.
post.description:
If the user is already in another voice channel, they are automatically
moved and a `VoiceStateUpdate(Left)` event is emitted for the old channel.

# Errors
- 401 if not authenticated.
- 403 if not a server member or plan limit exceeded.
- 404 if the channel does not exist.
- 422 if the channel is not a voice channel.
- 503 if voice is not configured (`LiveKit` disabled).

post.summary: Leave a voice channel. The user must be in the specified channel.
post.description:
# Errors
- 401 if not authenticated.
- 409 if the user is not in the specified channel.
- 503 if voice is not configured (`LiveKit` disabled).

get.summary: List all participants currently in a voice channel.
get.description:
# Errors
- 401 if not authenticated.
- 403 if not a server member.
- 404 if the channel does not exist.
- 503 if voice is not configured (`LiveKit` disabled).

get.summary: List DM conversations for the authenticated user.
get.description:
Returns DM conversations sorted by most recent activity (message or creation).
Uses cursor-based pagination.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if the cursor is invalid or a repository error occurs.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp cursor -- fetch DMs with last activity before this time.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of DMs to return (1-100, default 50).

post.summary: Create a new DM conversation or return an existing one (idempotent).
post.description:
If a DM already exists between the caller and the recipient, returns the
existing conversation with `200 OK`. Otherwise creates a new DM and returns
`201 Created`.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

delete.description:
Removes the authenticated user from the DM server. The conversation data
is preserved for the other participant.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on authorization failure or repository error.

403.description: Not a member of this DM

get.summary: SSE event stream — delivers real-time events to the authenticated user.
get.description:
The stream filters events based on:
1. **Server scope**: only events for servers the user is a member of.
2. **User scope**: user-targeted events (DMs, bans) only for this user.
3. **Sender exclusion**: message events skip the sender (client has
   optimistic UI).

**Dynamic `server_ids`**: The filter set is updated in-flight when the user
joins/leaves a server or a DM is created targeting them. This eliminates
the need for client-side reconnects on membership changes.

The stream pipeline has two stages:
- **Stage 1 (intercept)**: `.map()` — detects membership-change events
  affecting this user and updates the `watch` channel.
- **Stage 2 (filter + serialize)**: `.filter_map()` — reads the latest
  `server_ids` from the `watch` channel to filter and serialize events.

On reconnect, the client invalidates all queries (ADR-SSE-006) — no
event buffering or `Last-Event-ID` replay needed.

**Presence lifecycle**: On connect, the user is marked online in the
`PresenceTracker` and a `PresenceChanged` event is emitted. A heartbeat
stream calls `touch()` every 30s so the background sweep (in main.rs)
knows the connection is still alive.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if the user's server list cannot be fetched.

get.summary: Preview an invite by code (no authentication required).
get.description:
Returns the server name and member count so a user can decide whether to join.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if the invite is not found or a repository error occurs.

get.summary: Fetch a pre-key bundle for a target user (Olm session establishment).
get.description:
Atomically claims one one-time key (or falls back to the fallback key).

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if the user has no registered devices or on repository error.

get.summary: Get the count of remaining non-fallback one-time keys for the caller's device.
get.description:
Clients use this to decide when to upload more one-time keys.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: Device ID to check key count for

post.summary: Register a device with its identity and signing keys.
post.description:
Upserts on (`user_id`, `device_id`) -- re-registering replaces existing keys.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

delete.summary: Remove a device and its associated keys.
delete.description:
Only the device owner can remove their own devices.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if the device does not exist or on repository error.

get.summary: List all registered devices for a user.

post.summary: Upload one-time keys (and/or fallback keys) for a device.
post.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

get.summary: Get the authenticated user's preferences.
get.description:
Returns default preferences (`dndEnabled: false`) when no explicit setting exists.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on repository error.

patch.summary: Update the authenticated user's preferences (partial patch).
patch.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

post.summary: Update the authenticated user's presence status.
post.description:
Validates that the requested status is user-settable (not `offline`),
then emits a `PresenceChanged` event via the event bus so other
connected users receive the update through SSE.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on invalid status value.

get.summary: Get the authenticated user's own profile.
get.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if the profile is not found or a repository error occurs.

patch.summary: Update the authenticated user's profile fields (avatar, display name, custom status).
patch.description:
Patch semantics: only provided fields are updated; omitted fields remain unchanged.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

get.summary: List all servers the authenticated user is a member of.

post.summary: Create a new server.
post.description:
The authenticated user becomes the owner. A default `#general` channel
and server membership are created automatically.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

get.summary: Get a server by ID.
get.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if the server is not found or a repository error occurs.

delete.summary: Delete a server. Requires owner role.
delete.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on insufficient role, not found, or repository error.

patch.summary: Update a server's name. Requires admin+ role.
patch.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

get.summary: List bans for a server with cursor-based pagination. Requires admin+ role.
get.description:
Use `before` (ISO 8601) to paginate backward. Default limit is 50, max is 100.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if the cursor is invalid, authorization fails, or a repository error occurs.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp cursor -- fetch bans created before this time.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of bans to return (1-100, default 50).

post.summary:
Ban a user from a server and remove their membership.
Requires admin+ role with hierarchy enforcement.
post.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on authorization failure, conflict, or repository error.

403.description: Insufficient role / hierarchy violation / cannot ban owner

delete.summary: Unban a user from a server. Requires admin+ role.
delete.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on authorization failure or repository error.

get.summary: List all channels in a server.

post.summary: Create a new channel in a server. Requires admin+ role.
post.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

delete.summary: Delete a channel. Requires admin+ role.
delete.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if this is the last channel or the channel is not found.

patch.summary:
Update a channel's name, topic, and/or flags. Requires admin+ role.
Enabling encryption requires owner role (one-way toggle).
patch.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

post.summary: Create a new invite for a server.
post.description:
The authenticated user must be a member of the server. Returns a shareable
invite code that can be used by others to join.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure, permission denial, or repository error.

post.summary: Leave a server voluntarily. The owner cannot leave (must transfer ownership first).
post.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure or repository error.

404.description: Server not found or not a member

get.summary: List members of a server with cursor-based pagination.
get.description:
Use `before` (ISO 8601) to paginate backward. Default limit is 50, max is 100.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` if the cursor is invalid or a repository error occurs.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp cursor -- fetch members who joined before this time.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of members to return (1-100, default 50).

post.summary: Join a server via an invite code.
post.description:
Validates the invite, checks that the user is not already a member,
and adds them to the server.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on invalid invite, expired invite, or conflict.

delete.summary: Kick a member from a server. Requires moderator+ role with hierarchy enforcement.
delete.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on authorization failure or repository error.

patch.summary: Assign a role to a server member. Requires admin+ role with hierarchy enforcement.
patch.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure, authorization failure, or repository error.

get.summary: Get moderation settings for a server.
get.description:
Returns current Tier 2 category toggles and informational tier lists.
Any server member can read settings.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on authorization failure or repository error.

patch.summary: Update moderation settings for a server.
patch.description:
Replaces Tier 2 category toggles. Requires server owner role.

# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on validation failure, authorization failure, or repository error.

post.summary: Transfer server ownership. Only the current owner can do this.
post.description:
# Errors
Returns `ApiError` on authorization failure or repository error.

404.description: Server or new owner not found

post.summary:
Heartbeat to keep a voice session alive. Clients should call this
periodically (e.g. every 30s) while connected to voice.
post.description:
The `session_id` in the request body must match the session created on join.
This prevents a stale device from keeping a replaced session alive.

# Errors
- 401 if not authenticated.
- 404 if the session does not exist (expired, replaced, or user not in voice).
- 503 if voice is not configured (`LiveKit` disabled).

post.summary: Refresh a `LiveKit` JWT for an active voice session.
post.description:
Unlike `join_voice`, this does NOT replace the server-side session or emit
SSE leave/join events. Other participants see no change. The client uses
this to extend its `LiveKit` connection without UI churn.

# Errors
- 401 if not authenticated.
- 404 if the channel or session does not exist (expired/swept).
- 422 if the channel is not a voice channel.
- 503 if voice is not configured (`LiveKit` disabled).

AddReactionRequest.description: Request body for adding a reaction.

AssignRoleRequest.description: Request body for assigning a role to a server member.

role.description: The role to assign (admin, moderator, member). Use transfer-ownership for owner.

BanListQuery.description: Query parameters for listing bans (cursor-based pagination).

before.description: ISO 8601 timestamp cursor -- fetch bans created before this time.

limit.description: Maximum number of bans to return (1-100, default 50).

BanListResponse.description: Envelope for a list of server bans with cursor pagination (ADR-036).

nextCursor.description: Cursor for the next page. `None` if this is the last page.

BanResponse.description: Server ban response returned to API consumers.

BanUserRequest.description: Request body for banning a user from a server.

CategoryId.description: Unique identifier for a channel category.

ChannelId.description: Unique identifier for a channel within a server.

ChannelListResponse.description: Envelope for a list of channels (ADR-036).

ChannelResponse.description: Channel response returned to API consumers.

encrypted.description: Whether Megolm E2EE is enabled on this channel.

slowModeSeconds.description: Minimum seconds between messages per user. 0 = disabled.

username.description: The username to check for availability.

CheckUsernameResponse.description: Response for the username availability check.

available.description: Whether the username is available for registration.

ClaimedKeyResponse.description: A claimed key in a pre-key bundle response.

CreateChannelRequest.description: Request body for creating a new channel.

isPrivate.description: Whether the channel is private (visible only to admin+ or explicit role grants).

isReadOnly.description: Whether the channel is read-only (only admin+ can post messages).

CreateDesktopAuthRequest.description: Request body for creating a desktop auth code.

refreshToken.description: Supabase refresh token to store for the desktop app.

CreateDesktopAuthResponse.description: Response containing the one-time auth code.

CreateDmRequest.description: Request body for creating a new DM conversation.

recipientId.description: The user ID of the recipient.

CreateInviteRequest.description: Request body for creating a new invite.

expiresInHours.description: Invite expires after this many hours. `None` means it never expires.

maxUses.description: Maximum number of times this invite can be used. `None` means unlimited.

CreateMegolmSessionRequest.description: Request body for registering a Megolm session on a channel.

sessionId.description: The vodozemac Megolm session ID (base64-encoded Ed25519 public key).

CreateServerRequest.description: Request body for creating a new server.

DeviceId.description: Device identifier (client-generated string, not a UUID).

DeviceKeyId.description: Unique identifier for a device key record.

DeviceListResponse.description: Envelope for a list of devices.

DmLastMessageResponse.description: Last message preview embedded in DM list items.

encrypted.description: Whether this message contains E2EE ciphertext.

DmListItem.description: A single DM conversation in the list response.

DmListQuery.description: Query parameters for listing DMs (cursor-based pagination).

before.description: ISO 8601 timestamp cursor -- fetch DMs with last activity before this time.

limit.description: Maximum number of DMs to return (1-100, default 50).

DmListResponse.description: Envelope for a list of DM conversations with cursor pagination (ADR-036).

nextCursor.description: Cursor for the next page. `None` if this is the last page.

DmRecipientResponse.description: Recipient profile embedded in DM responses.

DmResponse.description: Response for a single DM conversation (create or get).

EditMessageRequest.description: Request body for editing a message.

HealthResponse.description: Deep health check response with component status.

latencyMs.description: Time taken for health check in milliseconds

InviteCode.description: Invite code for joining a server.

InvitePreviewResponse.description:
Public invite preview (no auth required).

Contains just enough information for a user to decide whether to join.

InviteResponse.description: Invite response returned to API consumers.

JoinServerRequest.description: Request body for joining a server via invite code.

status.description: Always `"ok"` when the process is alive.

MarkReadRequest.description: Request body for marking a channel as read.

lastMessageId.description: ID of the last message the user has read.

MegolmSessionId.description: Unique identifier for a Megolm session record.

MegolmSessionResponse.description: Response after registering a Megolm session.

MemberListQuery.description: Query parameters for listing members (cursor-based pagination).

before.description: ISO 8601 timestamp cursor -- fetch members who joined before this time.

limit.description: Maximum number of members to return (1-100, default 50).

MemberListResponse.description: Envelope for a list of server members with cursor pagination (ADR-036).

nextCursor.description: Cursor for the next page. `None` if this is the last page.

MemberResponse.description: Server member response returned to API consumers.

MessageListQuery.description: Query parameters for listing messages (cursor-based pagination).

before.description: ISO 8601 timestamp cursor — fetch messages created before this time.

limit.description: Maximum number of messages to return (1-100, default 50).

MessageListResponse.description: Envelope for a list of messages with cursor pagination (ADR-036).

nextCursor.description: Cursor for the next page. `None` if this is the last page.

MessageResponse.description:
Message response returned to API consumers.

Soft-deleted messages are filtered from list queries, but `deleted_by`
is included so realtime-delivered tombstones can distinguish self-deletes
from moderator-deletes on the client.

oneOf.description:
WHO deleted this message. `None` for live messages; `Some` for
soft-deleted messages delivered via realtime.

encrypted.description: Whether this message contains E2EE ciphertext.

moderatedAt.description: When `AutoMod` flagged this message. Content is already masked.

oneOf.description: Preview of the parent message (author + content snippet).
Parent message ID when this is a reply.

reactions.description: Aggregated reaction summaries for this message.

senderDeviceId.description: Device ID of the sender. Present when `encrypted = true`.

systemEventKey.description: System event key (e.g. `member_join`). Only present for system messages.

MessageType.description:
Discriminates user messages from system announcements.

`Default` = regular user message. `System` = server-generated event
(join, leave, etc.) whose display text is resolved from `system_event_key`
on the frontend via i18n templates.

categories.description: Current Tier 2 category toggles for this server.

tier2Available.description: Available Tier 2 categories the admin can toggle.

OneTimeKeyDto.description: A single one-time key in an upload batch.

isFallback.description: Whether this is a fallback key (persists until rotated).

OneTimeKeyId.description: Unique identifier for a one-time key record.

ParentMessagePreview.description:
Lightweight preview of a parent message for reply display.

Sent alongside reply messages so the client can render a quote block
without an extra API call.

contentPreview.description: First 100 characters of the parent message content.

deleted.description:
WHY: When true, the parent message was soft-deleted. The frontend
renders "[Original message was deleted]" instead of the content.
`author_username` and `content_preview` are empty when deleted
to avoid leaking content or identity.

PreKeyBundleResponse.description: Pre-key bundle response for Olm session establishment.

ProblemDetails.description:
RFC 9457 Problem Details response.

All API errors are returned in this standardized format.

upgrade_url.description:
WHY: Drives upsell when a plan limit is exceeded. The frontend renders
an "Upgrade" CTA using this URL. Only present on `LimitExceeded` errors.

ProfileResponse.description: Profile response returned to API consumers.

ReactionSummary.description: Aggregated reaction summary for a single emoji on a message.

RedeemDesktopAuthRequest.description: Request body for redeeming a desktop auth code.

authCode.description: The one-time auth code received via deep link.

codeVerifier.description: The PKCE `code_verifier` (plaintext; S256-hashed must match stored `code_challenge`).

RefreshVoiceTokenRequest.description: Request body for the voice token refresh endpoint.

sessionId.description:
The session identifier returned when the user joined voice.
The server derives the channel from the session — no client-supplied
`channel_id` to prevent IDOR (minting tokens for the wrong room).

RefreshVoiceTokenResponse.description:
Response for a token refresh — lighter than `VoiceTokenResponse` because
the session is not replaced (no `session_id`, no `previous_channel_id`).

ttlSecs.description: Token TTL in seconds. Frontend schedules the next refresh at 80%.

RegisterDeviceRequest.description: Request body for registering a device with its identity keys.

Role.description:
Strongly-typed role enum matching the `server_members.role` TEXT column.

Variants are ordered by authority level (Member < Moderator < Admin < Owner).
`PartialOrd`/`Ord` are manually avoided to prevent accidental comparisons;
use `level()` or `can_moderate()` for hierarchy checks.

SendMessageRequest.description: Request body for sending a new message.

content.description: Message content (required, non-empty). Contains ciphertext when `encrypted = true`.

encrypted.description: Whether this message contains E2EE ciphertext. Defaults to `false`.

oneOf.description: Parent message ID for reply threading. Omit for top-level messages.

senderDeviceId.description: Device ID of the sending device. Required when `encrypted = true`.

ServerListResponse.description: Envelope for a list of servers (ADR-036).

ServerResponse.description: Server response returned to API consumers.

TransferOwnershipRequest.description: Request body for transferring server ownership.

newOwnerId.description: The user ID of the new owner (must be an existing member).

UpdateChannelRequest.description: Request body for updating an existing channel.

encrypted.description: Enable Megolm E2EE (one-way toggle: once true, cannot be set back to false).

slowModeSeconds.description: Minimum seconds between messages per user. 0 = disable slow mode. Admin+ only.

topic.description: New channel topic (if provided; null clears it).

UpdateModerationSettingsRequest.description: Request body for updating server moderation settings (replace semantics).

categories.description: Full desired state of Tier 2 category toggles.

UpdateNotificationSettingsRequest.description: Request body for updating notification settings.

UpdatePresenceRequest.description: Request body for updating presence status.

status.description:
User-settable status. Must be one of: `online`, `idle`, `dnd`.
`offline` is system-managed (set on disconnect) and cannot be set via this endpoint.

UpdateProfileRequest.description: Request body for updating the authenticated user's profile.

UpdateServerRequest.description: Request body for updating a server.

UpdateUserPreferencesRequest.description: Request body for updating user preferences (partial patch).

UploadOneTimeKeysRequest.description: Request body for uploading one-time keys for a device.

deviceId.description: Device identifier these keys belong to.

keys.description: Batch of one-time keys to upload.

UserId.description: Unique identifier for a user (Supabase auth.users UUID).

VoiceAction.description: Voice channel action for SSE events.

VoiceHeartbeatRequest.description: Request body for the voice heartbeat endpoint.

isActive.description:
Whether the user is actively participating (speaking, unmuted).
`None` means the client didn't send a value; handler treats that as
`true` via `unwrap_or(true)` for backward compatibility.

isMuted.description:
Whether the user's microphone is muted. Muted users are still
considered "active" for AFK purposes (they are listening).

sessionId.description:
The session identifier returned when the user joined voice.
Ensures only the current device's session receives the heartbeat.

VoiceParticipantResponse.description: A single voice participant in a channel.

displayName.description:
Display name (nickname or username). Populated from the user's
profile — not stored on `VoiceSession` itself.

VoiceParticipantsResponse.description: Envelope for a list of voice participants (ADR-036).

VoiceTokenResponse.description: Voice token response returned after joining a voice channel.

oneOf.description:
Channel the user was previously in (if auto-moved). Clients use this
to update UI state for the old channel.

sessionId.description:
Opaque session identifier. Clients must send this back in heartbeats
so the server can distinguish the current device from stale ones.

token.description: `LiveKit` JWT token for the client to connect with.

ttlSecs.description: Token time-to-live in seconds. Frontend schedules refresh at 80% of this.

tags.name: Keys
tags.description: E2EE key distribution (device keys, pre-key bundles)
